what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard document that introduces a client brief for a phone-driven annotation/feedback system, intended as shared context for research agents; it lists an ordered sequence of related board items and records edit history.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, address, type=document, source=claude_code, state=draft, title, author session, channel, and an order list of 17 board:// items
Body describes a CTX/MSG/BD schema but only contains an introductory paragraph; the actual CTX, MSG, and BD sections are not present
History shows transitions through draft, order, and title states with notes like 'sequence brief blocks', 'append UI-feedback message', and 'short-lead title'
Created and updated timestamps are about 31 minutes apart, indicating rapid iterative editing
No code symbols are present in the file
standouts: The order array explicitly sequences 17 related noticeboard items
History notes reveal iterative reordering and titling rather than content expansion
The body declares a structured CTX/MSG/BD breakdown but does not include it
conventions: Follows the channel-memory noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema
Uses board:// URI identifiers for cross-item references
Markdown document with metadata, body, and history sections
Draft state with source attribution to claude_code
concerns: The file promises CTX/MSG/BD sections in the body but fails to provide them, so it is incomplete as a standalone brief
The long order list references many external items whose contents are not shown here
History entries mention content edits, but the visible body does not reflect the implied sections
notes: A draft lead/index page for a phone-annotation client brief that sequences related noticeboard items but currently lacks the detailed CTX/MSG/BD sections it describes.
questions: What content is contained in each of the 17 ordered board items?
Where are the CTX, MSG, and BD sections promised in the body?
Is this file meant to be a table of contents or the full brief itself?
What is the 'UI-feedback message' that was appended?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to provide a shared context file with a full CTX/MSG/BD breakdown for research agents; actual content is only an introductory paragraph and an ordered list of related items, so the detailed breakdown is missing.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to assemble a multi-part client brief by sequencing related noticeboard items and providing a decomposition schema that separates context, raw client message, and detailed breakdown.
novelty: The file frames a client brief as a curated, ordered sequence of board items with a formal CTX/MSG/BD decomposition schema, though the schema is declared rather than populated.
contribution: Provides a top-level entry point and explicit ordering for a set of related brief items, plus a declared schema for decomposing client input into context, verbatim message, and detailed breakdown.
summary_for_embedding: Draft noticeboard item serving as a lead/index for a phone-driven annotation/feedback client brief, listing an ordered sequence of related board items and declaring a CTX/MSG/BD decomposition schema.
intention_for_embedding: To create a shared context document that organizes a client brief into context, raw message, and detailed breakdown sections for research agents.
